title: ByteWolf Camera Calibration v0.1
description: The measured intrinsics and lens distortion of a physical camera. It is a measurement, so it always carries measured provenance -- naming the method, when it was captured, and its reprojection error. It never carries a vendor spec or a guessed value; an uncalibrated camera has no document, not a document full of nulls. Loaded by brain/perception/camera_calibration.py, which is the only sanctioned path from a bench measurement into the twin.

camera_id.description: The twin camera this calibration is for, matching sensors.cameras in twin.yaml.

device.description: The physical camera, e.g. 'Hawkeye 4K Split V5'.

projection_model.description: 'fisheye' for a wide lens like the Hawkeye's 160-degree optic; a fisheye must be undistorted before any pinhole projection (target_estimator) consumes it.

image_size.description: The resolution the calibration was measured at. Intrinsics are only valid at this size; they scale with resolution.

intrinsics.description: The pinhole camera matrix in pixels. For a fisheye, these are the fisheye model's fx/fy/cx/cy, valid only with the distortion coefficients below.

model.description: 'kannala_brandt' is OpenCV's fisheye model (4 coefficients). 'plumb_bob'/'rational_polynomial' are pinhole models. 'none' only for an ideal, distortion-free render.

coefficients.description: Distortion coefficients in the model's order; empty only for model 'none'.

reprojection_error_px.description: RMS reprojection error of the calibration, in pixels. A high value means the calibration is untrustworthy; the loader is where a project-chosen threshold is enforced.

provenance.description: A calibration is always a measurement; this records how and when.

captured_at.description: RFC 3339, UTC, with an explicit offset.

target.description: The calibration target used, e.g. a checkerboard spec.

then.description: Model 'none' means no distortion, so it carries no coefficients.

else.description: Any real distortion model must carry at least one coefficient.
